I had a great experience working with The Masters Law Firm after my vehicle was involved in an accident. Kelly Elswick-Hall handled my case and was outstanding from start to finish. She clearly explained the process, set expectations, and made sure I understood every step.

Kelly worked diligently to secure a strong outcome and ensured everything was handled thoroughly and professionally. Communication was excellent — I was kept informed throughout the entire process.

I truly appreciate Kelly’s responsiveness and dedication. I highly recommend The Masters Law Firm to anyone needing knowledgeable and reliable legal representation.

I began my relationship with the Masters Law Firm around 1994 as a result of an auto accident. I was treated with the utmost respect and concern. I really felt like the firm represented me exceptionally well. They also represented me in a couple of personal matters (resulting in favorable outcomes for me). Most recently, the firm represented me in another accident. My medical bills exceeded the insurance coverages. I wasn’t sure how to deal with that situation so I contacted Masters law again. My attorney, Kelly Elswick-Hall, and her paralegal, Rebecca Snodgrass, offered to represent me. I was not disappointed ! They immediately got to work dealing with medical providers bills, and the insurance companies. They kept me updated and informed along the way, and were very upbeat and positive. I feel that they worked hard to get my bills taken care of and even got some bills reduced. I was very pleased with the way they handled my case. Don’t call one of those big firms that advertise how big they are and how many clients they represent. That just means that they have lots of advertising fees and overhead costs… and you are just a number to them. I would strongly recommend the Masters Law Firm- give them a call first and get treated like a friend !
It is a great law firm. I highly recommend to go through them if you need anything.!!
Even though they could not take my case they were helpful and referred me to another lawyer that specialized in what I needed.
I would highly recommend any of the Attorneys at this firm , they are a truly caring team!

I was told I wouldn't hear from them in 2 weeks and instead I didn't hear from them for nearly a month. Found out through the insurance company that he "no longer had a lawyer". Today they confirmed the case was too small and they weren't taking it. I wish I had found out before Progressive did. I wish I had been told that weeks ago instead of wasting time with the car insurance company and getting my hopes up. I am very disappointed in the lack of communication and the run around I was given. Unless you're worth a fortune, go elsewhere.

reviews.text: My family went through a horrible experience with our insurance company. After a very bad storm, the roof on our home was damaged. We had water leaking into the interior of the house. The insurance company agreed to repair the inside but denied us on the roof. It made zero sense. The representative was extremely unprofessional and when we filed a complaint regarding the denial, he became personally vengeful and made false claims that caused the company to drop us.  We had made only one small claim in twenty years- always paid on time and a loyal customer. While having a big blue tarp across our roof, we were forced to find a new company to insure our home. It was difficult and stressful to say the least. My family suffered great financial burdens and hardship. We called Chad Love and were quickly put at ease. From the first phone call and meeting, we knew he would do all he could to help us. Chad utilized our documentation and went to work quickly. He is extremely knowledgeable about the insurance business and he took great care of my family! We cannot thank the Chad Love Law Firm enough for being there to assist our family. With three small children at the time, this was by far one of the most unsettling situations we had been in. Chad took the burden off of our shoulders completely. This titan of a man is my personal hero for the way he turned my case around.

The courts are not a kind place always, and this time when I had to go I found myself staring down the proverbial barrel of a gun in regards to my childs custody.

Three hearings in, and I didn't think I needed a lawyer until in the adjudicatory hearing. Then things took a definitive turn southwards.

To be honest my outlook on my prospects seemed extraordinarily bleak, and for a time there I really thought I had no hope.

Enter Mr. bayliss.

I called this man a week before my hearing began, tried to the best of my ability to explain the situation to him. And he said no problem we are going to get through this together.

I cannot recommend enough the services of bayliss law office.

Subsequently, after my father was involved in an accident at a major retail store, we found ourselves in need of guidance and representation, and Glenn and his team not only didn’t disappoint, they exceeded all expectations.

From day one we were treated with empathy, respect, and the utmost professionalism. Glenn and his staff were diligent and professional throughout every step of the litigation process. They ensured that we were supported, and informed with detailed transparency.

Complex cases can take months to years, but Glenn reached out personally several times ensuring everything was going according to timeline. His staff kept me updated on the progress of my case and worked diligently to ensure a timely resolution.

presentation of facts. Thanks to Glenn’s expertise, we were able to settle the case for fair compensation.

We are very grateful to Glenn, and his staff, for their tireless commitment in our case.  I highly recommend Glenn Martin Hammond Law Offices to anyone in need of legal representation.

They ensured that I was well-informed and supported, especially during the challenging period following my surgery.

Even amid the unprecedented challenges brought about by the Covid-19 pandemic, Glenn and his team demonstrated unwavering dedication. Despite court delays during this time, they kept me updated on the progress of my case and worked diligently to ensure a timely resolution.

Thanks to their expertise and tireless efforts, we were able to settle the case efficiently and fairly. I am deeply grateful for their commitment to securing the best possible outcome for me.

I wholeheartedly recommend Glenn Martin Hammond Law Offices to anyone in need of legal assistance. Their professionalism, compassion, and dedication truly set them apart.

He represented me as he would himself or his loved one, and that speaks volumes to not just how amazing of an attorney he is, but also a person.  He would always get back to me if not the same hour the same day when I reached out to him.  He wrote a 20 page motion to the court on the level of an english scholar.  When he called me personally and told me my case was dismissed it was the single greatest day of my life.  He definitely took my case on when he didn't have to, and for that I am forever grateful for him.  Trust me, you want this man on your side if he says he can help you - YOU WILL NOT REGRET IT.  Mr. Mowen - thank you for everything you did for me and ensuring that my future will not be jeopardized any longer.   YOU ARE THE BEST.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Our firm takes its professional and ethical obligations to clients very seriously.

In this matter, our firm’s involvement was brief. Like most law offices, trained staff members assist with client communication so that information can be provided promptly while attorneys supervise and remain responsible for all legal work.

If an attorney-client relationship is no longer productive or a client expresses dissatisfaction with representation, the professional rules governing attorneys may require withdrawal so the individual can seek other counsel.

Because we respect client confidentiality and our ethical duties, we cannot discuss the details of any representation in a public forum. We wish this individual the best moving forward.

Ms. Handley went as far as telling my children that she could recommend they get taken from me if they refuse to spend time with their abusive mother. Then on 10/1/19 my 12 year old daughter was again physically abused by her mother because she was forced by the court, upon Ms. Handley's recommendation, to be around her abusive mother. My daughter has refused to stay with her mother since that date. Today is 12/31/19. Ms. Handley ignored everything the children and I told her and did not do a proper investigation. She endangered my children by not properly doing her job. She wants $6,300 for what I call a catastrophic job. Skip this attorney and find one that actually cares. Note that my children who are old enough to choose where to live ALL live with me 24/7. This leaves my 7 year old in danger of being abused.

Update 12/7/21
On top of my earlier complaints, on 5/24/21 my children's mother got a DUI with one of our children in the car. Still wouldn't listen. She even told the children that if they didn't spend time with their mother, she would recommend they be taken from both of us and placed in foster care. I retained Jeremy Clark to represent me in a high-conflict custody case over the past five years. Initially, I believed his familiarity with my case would be an asset, but I was disappointed in how several key matters were handled.

In June 2023, my ex and I were court-ordered to use a co-parenting app, and he was awarded all long weekends. I immediately requested that “long weekends” be defined as school-scheduled holidays only. My concern was that my ex would manipulate sudden school closures due to weather or illness to claim additional time. My request went ignored, and for the past two years, this has been an ongoing issue. Whenever school abruptly closes, I’m pressured to give up my scheduled time—something that’s confusing and upsetting for a child who looks forward to our weekends together.

That same summer, my ex blocked communication between me and my son, and I went two weeks without contact. I emailed my Jeremy requesting he file for the right of first refusal, as my son was frequently being left with his stepmother for extended periods while his father traveled. That email also went unanswered. Jeremy did not follow up with me until nearly a year later in the summer of 2024, when a motion was filed against me.

Despite his advice to “dot our i’s and cross our t’s,” he failed to comply with a key statute involving my son’s therapist—who he had subpoenaed to testify on my behalf. Because he didn’t follow the proper legal procedure, the therapist was not permitted to testify. She was the only party there specifically for my son’s best interest, and she was silenced. I was never informed of this procedural mistake; I had to find it myself while researching the statutes to understand what went wrong.

After I received the court order that my son would no longer live with me primarily, Jeremy told me to “just enjoy my weekends and long summers with him.” He also advised me to inform my son about the possibility of moving so he’d speak to the GAL about his wishes. That advice was used against me in court.

I tried to retain new counsel mid-case, but no attorney would take over. I was told I had a “good attorney,” leaving me with no other choice at that moment.

My sincere advice: find an attorney outside of Boyd County who will communicate clearly, respond timely, stay up to date on family law statutes, and prioritize both you and your child’s best interests.

Please be cautious when seeking legal representation from this firm. There are many others that are quicker and charge less. My case is a small probate case, and although probate cases are lengthy in general, the amount of time it has taken to send simple letters is ridiculous. Keep in mind when you retain an attorney, YOU are paying high dollar for a service, so make sure you get your money's worth.
Very knowledgeable and friendly

I was astonished to learn the team I hired for probate representation did absolutely nothing to help my case. They completely ignored my legal, valid claims to the estate and seemed more interested in maintaining a cordial relationship with opposing counsel. They wrote one letter and then did nothing but send huge bills. Whenever I complained about their lack of effort and follow through, they said they had “many other clients” and made my concerns seem trivial. This firm and their probate lawyers are inexcusably callous and indifferent. I will be filing a malpractice claim because I suffered damages as a result of their incompetence. If you hire them, don’t expect to accomplish anything!

I requested to seek legal advice regarding “late fees” that were excessive and applied daily during pandemic. I was not requesting representation, just legal advice. I had spoke with Xavier W. Staggs regarding concern. He requested the name of owner or property manager. He asked multiple times, which then continued asking for details. After receiving all the information, he stated “I don’t know them and I can’t give you legal advice over the phone.”
Not even 20 minutes later, I receive a phone call from the landlord, and based on the conversation, and the manager stating “if it is because of the late fees, I can work with you.” I know he had contacted her, because I had spoke with her a couple days before as a follow up. So she has no reason to call right away again after I had informed her arrangement and details. I feel the reason for him contacting her, is because he saw it as a financial opportunity, considering he represents Realtors and property owners. After filing a complaint with the legal board. I receive a response from him, he contacted the landlord during the process of complaint and investigation and had her type, that she did not speak to him prior to that date.
Not only did he violate confidentiality rights, he committed malpractice and is using his benefits as a lawyer to cover up his  intentional mistake.
I highly do not recommend speaking with this lawyer or this specific firm about any information as he will contact the other party directly if it is more beneficial for himself and the firm.

It is a great privilege for me to express my gratitude  for everything Andrew Nason, attorney (I call him Andy), has done for me and my family.
Andy has been my advisor on numerous legal situations, for more than 20 years.  These legal situations have covered a multitude of situations---
from every day, common matters, to very complicated situations--such as legal aspects of research  being conducted at The Ohio State University.  My children have been told that Andy knows everything---and, I do mean everything--about me, and they should see him on any matters pertaining to me.

On a more personal side, Andy LISTENS! Whenever I meet with him, after a cordial greeting, he always asks, "what can I do for you"---he sincerely wants to know the purpose of my visit and how he can help.

Andy is "down to earth", and his legal education and background, is impeccable.  He knows how to "cut to the core' and help solve any, and all, legal situations that I have sought his help.

And, the office is not a flashy glass and chrome edifice.  You can tell that as soon as you enter the comfortable, small lobby.  The entire staff is very cordial---and, your feel like you are entering someone's living room for a cordial visit.

I highly recommend Andy Nason for anyone seeking legal advice.

I was audited by the IRS for the years 2009/2010/2011; They (IRS) said I owed them more than $31,000.00. Mr. Jones was friendly and very much a professional.   He met with the IRS on my behalf and I was due a refund of over $7,000.00 dollars. All I can say is I could not be more happy with the result. I met with Abe and Hoyt in April 2024 regarding a malicious assault I sustained by drunken off duty Huntington Police Corporal Daniel Mulvaney, followed by a wrongful arrest for Disorderly and Obstruction. All my charges were immediately dismissed. And it’s all on video. Abe collected my video evidence. Later, I filed a FOIA request with the city and received 2 flash drives of falsified documents and videos exonerating me. Abe collected that evidence as well. Every time I called Abe, I had to leave a message. I also sent Abe emails asking for updates. On the date the statute of limitations expired, Abe emailed me to tell me he couldn’t help me. Abe sat on my evidence for over 10 months. Abe didn’t help me, and Abe made sure no one else could either. If Abe would have told me the truth from the beginning, that he couldn’t help me, I would not have handed over all of the video evidence and FOIA documents. I would have found another attorney to help. I believe Abe took advantage of my disability and vulnerability to exhaust the statute of limitations on my case. I would give zero stars if possible. Playing with a victim’s life is nasty work.

Edited to respond to Abe: Everything I stated is true and factual. I’m entitled to my opinion and you’re entitled to yours. If you think I defamed you, sue me.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Leep:

Your review is inaccurate and contains the statutory elements of libel - written defamation.

Our reputation at Glazer Saad Anderson L.C. precedes your inflammatory and grossly inaccurate review.

We wish you the best of luck moving forward.

reviews.ownerResponse: Our firm's decision to take a case depends on many factors. After you acknowledged our firm's commitment to Black History Month, you make a very serious accusation against us based on unsupported assumptions, and, incredibly without our having performed any legal services for you. In short, you haven't assigned us a rating based on our services, but rather your perceived slight from our having decided that we could not represent you. We wish you every success in your case, and hope that moving forward you will not be so quick to judge others based on assumptions.

My name is Shannon Smith Buchko, I went to Jerry Alford simply bc he was close by and my daddy has worked with him before over land deeds and some other similar things. I also thought that being a local all my life couldn't hurt. What I needed was a lawyer to just go to court and speak for me so I wouldn't mess all I had accomplished up. I am not a lawyer and I do not speak the language of court. I only seem to make things worse or misunderstood when I do so. I did not want to chance it since I was fighting for custody of my 2 daughters. So when I went to him I (like an idiot) told him how much money I had and ask if he could/would help me? He said yes and so I hired him. As time is drawing near to court date l find myself doing all the work such as getting/ gathering all past court papers and finding what he needs, having to go and get cds of transcripts as well and going through them. I wasn't to upset with it bc I wanted it done right and I was familiar. The court date arrived and he showed up as promised and brought past records and had obviously went over them along with my notes. I did get full custody of both of my daughters and since I won it seemed that it's a given the winning attorney draws up the order for the judge to sign. On DEC 07 I received a paper stating that I was awarded full custody but before my birthday on DEC 13 I received another stating it was amended due to the findings of the fact (meaning why I got full custody) was left out. when I got the paper and found out what finding of the fact meant and that it was supposed to be in the papers Mr. Alford was to turn in to the court I went to him and showed him what I had gotten and ask him to please put the findings of the fact in the order he stated he would need 1800$ to do so. I did not have 1800$ I gave him ALL I had and he knew it. I could not believe what I was hearing. Needless to say I could have burnt my money in the back yard and gotten more out of it. The order was later overturned bc I had no lawyer and no money. Mr Alford wouldn't even work with me on a payment plan when I came back later once I figured out I couldn't do it. So before you hire Jerry Alford from Alum Creek to help you out in your time of need ask yourself just how important that need to you is bc if it's important to you then trust someone who has been there and keep looking. Hope this helps someone so they'll never be where I was or feel the helplessness I felt and what it cost me and I'm not talking about money. God bless

I can’t say enough good things about Sebastian Joy. From the very beginning, he treated my case with seriousness, professionalism, and respect. He took the time to explain every step of the process, answered all of my questions, and made sure I understood what was happening at every stage.

What stood out most was how prepared and confident Sebastian was. He clearly knew the law, knew the strategy, and fought hard to make sure my side of the story was heard. During one of the most stressful times of my life, he stayed calm, focused, and reassuring.

If you are looking for someone who is knowledgeable, responsive, and truly committed to defending their clients, I highly recommend Sebastian Joy. Having the right attorney matters, and I’m extremely grateful for the work he put into my case.

I dealt with Pete a year who was my lawyer during a divorce, my ex’s lawyer ate his breakfast, lunch and dinner. I felt intimidated by my ex and her lawyer the entire time, we paid a significant amount of money and the only thing in the end that Happened was I made my ex’s deal and she got exactly what she wanted 100%! I could have saved myself a significant amount of money and time if I would have made that same deal a year prior. I lost my house, got no equity payment for it etc I didn’t even get paperwork of who bought my house or for how much! Every time I went to Pete’s office you couldn’t keep his attention longer than 3 minutes due to constant interruptions. My ex’s lawyer was tech savvy, this intimidated Pete as Pete is not tech savvy at all, this was detrimental in my case as my ex’s lawyer knew Pete was not tech savvy and used it against him, Pete did nothing but play defense and even allowed my ex’s lawyer to back out of a preliminary agreement we made prior. She got what she wanted and then told the judge no agreement was made. In the end… Pete was looked at by the Cabell county court and my ex’s lawyer as an old guy from Boone County, now I have to pay for the poor decisions and poor legal guidance I received.

Pete takes time to get to know his clients and fights hard for them! He is a dedicated attorney and wants to help others. His experience is unmatched in this area. If you want a truly experienced attorney that cares and listens, you should contact Pete!

A couple of years ago I had an issue with the partial ownership of gas wells in Mingo County, WVA. I live in west Tennessee so had no idea of an attorney in that area who might help me. After an Internet search I contacted Mo Persinger, and after a telephone interview, retained him to change the entity under which the ownership of those well shares was recorded.
He thoroughly knows his business and after a short period of time had my problem solved. Throughout this process either he or his assistant, Robin, kept me thoroughly informed. I thought the costs for their services were reasonable for the services performed, and should I ever need other legal services in that area, Persinger and Persinger would be my first contact.

reviews.text: Talk to Rick about real estate.He was an arrogant prick tbh. Said good luck and hung up on me.

Rick Faulkner is a family friend of mine and their relationship goes back decades. When I needed an attorney recently I called Rick, as he has represented me in the past. However, he assigned a lawyer at the firm that I had only heard of, Robert Trujillo, to my case this time. And did he deliver.

Rob and Rick are cut from the same cloth and he made a wise choice having Rob come work for the firm. Rob made me feel like I was in the best hands and he was extremely thorough in building his case for me. To say Rob understands his craft is an understatement; he’s passionate about being a good lawyer. He lives for it and it shows. And that’s the kind of guy you want on your side. This is an attorney who will work from home and give you a call on a Saturday morning and talk to you about your case. He truly put his best effort forward to help me and made an effort to reassure me of any misgivings I had about the case. I have no doubt that Rick is proud to have him represent the law firm.

On the day of court Rob showed up prepared and armed to the teeth with his well-crafted arguments. Needless to say, we had a favorable outcome in regards to my case and for that I am very grateful.

I am positive that in the future an attorney will have to be needed for certain things in my life that will pop up. I am not going with anyone else for that role except the Faulkner Law Firm. You’d be wise to do the same. They will not disappoint.

Much appreciation Rick and Rob! I owe you one.
Best law firm in town, hands down!  If you're going for Rick and he is too busy, try Robert! The girls in the office are extremely nice and very helpful. Overall, a great staff!

reviews.text: Whatever you do, do not use this law firm.  They lie to cover their misrepresentations and won’t contact you timely.  I filed bankruptcy and was on my parent’s account to assist with bills . Parent is elderly and they took her money paid off my debt and still left me in bankruptcy.  It was mishandled.  Waited 5 months to even tell me what was going on, I thought it was over since I received a letter from the bankruptcy court stating the discharge was complete and that was April.  Then in August of this year contacted and said I owed them money and they were gonna take it from my parent.  Then sent me a letter November 6 and only gave us 4 days to pay.  Whatever you do, do not use them!!! The bankruptcy was filed in October 2022 and a year later August 2023  laid it out this way.  I ended up pushed back in debt owing more than was filed cause I have to pay back the money they took which was 6000 more than I filed.   The whole reason I filed was I got sick and this isn’t helping my mental health at all but they don’t care as long as their butts are covered.  I advised my attorney I was on said accounts three different times but she lied and said I didn’t, but I did.  Then told me I would be contacted (this was August) in two weeks after they spoke to the trustee.  She didn’t I waited to hear and just received a letter in the mail like I said giving us 4 days to pay.  Not trustworthy and very bad representation.  Just Beware and proceed with caution if you do decide to use them.
This place is awful don’t go here. Over priced for poor quality service. You won’t get your free 30 minutes here. Go someplace else!

reviews.text: Jay Gerber reached out to me through a law firm referral process for a case for my daughter. His representation is top-notch. Jay is very professional and very personable. He explained things to my young daughter in a way she could understand and made sure she understood what was going on with her case. He kept me updated every step of the way and made us both feel very comfortable. He was always very straightforward and honest, no BS, which is something I truly appreciate. Jay always had my and my daughter's best interests at heart. He always returned my calls and texts in a very timely manner. I never had to hunt him down for an update. Even though he had to drive over and hour just to sit and wait most of our court dates, Jay was always on time for court, even showed up early to meet privately with us, every single time. He waited with us no matter how long it took to be called in to see the judge. He always made small talk with us and we often exchanged funny stories as we waited. It meant a lot to both of us that he was always so genuinely kind. I personally would recommend Jay Gerber as an attorney for any type of legal matter. If there were 10 stars to choose for his legal advice and services he provided, he's a solid 11! Thank you Jay for everything!

Look elsewhere. Terrible experience with Justin Raber as a divorce lawyer. Wouldn't fight for me, wouldn't do the things he promised even in the courtroom, and wouldn't answer simple questions (even face to face) or call back. He also didn't tell me until our final meet that there was a major conflict of interest in who he associates with and my case. His lack of care for me as a client was evident early on. I have the evidence to prove this, to include court tapes I paid for, making promises he later declined to fulfill, as well as emails and phone calls to his office that were documented. One in particular where I was told if I wanted to speak to him, I could try and catch him at the court house. That's after paying a retainer and being told he won't be making a call back and I can go find him. In another, I was told I didn't need to appear but did so anyway and opposing counsel was all there and setup with their client. Had him as a lawyer for 8 months and I saw him 5 times. Once at retaining, once at the window of his office in coincidence, in a hearing where he did better representing the opposition, in another court appearance where he couldn't articulate the one point I'd stressed many times and made clear from the beginning as a simple yes or no question, and lastly to get answers why he was doing so badly to determine if I'd keep him. You won't reach him either, so you'll talk to a secretary every time and he doesn't call back. I have my new lawyers cell and can call directly or text and his record keeping and sharing of official documents is actually present to his clients, where that was absent with Rabers office. Opposing counsel also claimed to fail in getting important documents he was supposed to send that I had provided previously, long before firing him. Got another lawyer and almost immediately got the life changing things my children and I were asking of Raber, but didn't receive. Everything he had a hand in seems to have been botched and it's extremely dissapointing. Rabers inadequacies have even been seen since and brought up in motions since his removal where it was observed he didn't follow up or do what he promised. Filing a formal complaint against Justin Raber, with proof of complete lack of care for my case and my children unless he reaches out and makes it right. Will also share my experience with those in the community. I cannot ethically recommend him to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We're sorry to hear you feel this way.

As attorneys, we are held to professional and ethical standards that include respecting confidentiality and refraining from discussing the details of any client’s case in a public forum. While we cannot share specifics, we can say that we work hard to advocate for our clients while also managing expectations based on the facts, applicable law, and the reality of the legal process.

We recognize that going through a divorce is emotionally difficult, and it's not uncommon for tensions to run high. That said, we are committed to clear communication, professional representation, and doing what is legally and ethically appropriate in each case.

If you still have concerns you would like to address directly, we are open to a respectful and constructive conversation offline. Our goal is always to serve our clients with honesty and professionalism, even when that may involve difficult truths or outcomes that are outside our control.

We wish you and your family well moving forward.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear CJ,

Thank you for your comment. It is important to clarify a few points to ensure accurate information is shared.

First, Mr. Raber’s professional career and his service on the Wood County Board of Education are separate roles. His professional responsibilities as part of his career are distinct from his service on the Board of Education. We encourage everyone to consider the facts when evaluating his contributions in each capacity.

Second, we note that you are not a client of our firm, and your comments appear to stem from a personal vendetta rather than genuine concerns. While we respect everyone’s right to express their opinions, we kindly ask that discussions remain fact-based and constructive, particularly when addressing issues that affect the community.

We remain committed to serving our clients and the community with integrity. Should you have any factual concerns or questions, we encourage you to address them through appropriate channels.

Sincerely,
Raber Law Offices, PLLC

Please be cautious when seeking legal representation from this firm. There are many others that are quicker and charge less. My case is a small probate case, and although probate cases are lengthy in general, the amount of time it has taken to send simple letters is ridiculous. Keep in mind when you retain an attorney, YOU are paying high dollar for a service, so make sure you get your money's worth.

I was astonished to learn the team I hired for probate representation did absolutely nothing to help my case. They completely ignored my legal, valid claims to the estate and seemed more interested in maintaining a cordial relationship with opposing counsel. They wrote one letter and then did nothing but send huge bills. Whenever I complained about their lack of effort and follow through, they said they had “many other clients” and made my concerns seem trivial. This firm and their probate lawyers are inexcusably callous and indifferent. I will be filing a malpractice claim because I suffered damages as a result of their incompetence. If you hire them, don’t expect to accomplish anything!

I requested to seek legal advice regarding “late fees” that were excessive and applied daily during pandemic. I was not requesting representation, just legal advice. I had spoke with Xavier W. Staggs regarding concern. He requested the name of owner or property manager. He asked multiple times, which then continued asking for details. After receiving all the information, he stated “I don’t know them and I can’t give you legal advice over the phone.”
Not even 20 minutes later, I receive a phone call from the landlord, and based on the conversation, and the manager stating “if it is because of the late fees, I can work with you.” I know he had contacted her, because I had spoke with her a couple days before as a follow up. So she has no reason to call right away again after I had informed her arrangement and details. I feel the reason for him contacting her, is because he saw it as a financial opportunity, considering he represents Realtors and property owners. After filing a complaint with the legal board. I receive a response from him, he contacted the landlord during the process of complaint and investigation and had her type, that she did not speak to him prior to that date.
Not only did he violate confidentiality rights, he committed malpractice and is using his benefits as a lawyer to cover up his  intentional mistake.
I highly do not recommend speaking with this lawyer or this specific firm about any information as he will contact the other party directly if it is more beneficial for himself and the firm.

From start to finish Jim and his team has been amazing to work with. They far surpassed my expectations. I couldn’t be happier.
Jim Leach is a very open and honest man. I truly appreciate him and his office for what they have done for me. I would recommend him to friends and family.

I have been working with Jim Leach and his staff for 3 years now and I'm so thankfull they do all the work for me just as he told me he would the day I come to him. I had a car accident a few years ago and Jim Leach along with ALL the staff takes care of all the phone calls, transferance of records, medical bills coming in, tracking all information related to my case, and while keeping me informed or asking a question occasionally I have not had to deal with any of the headaches that come with trying to get an insurance company for large companies/corporations to work with me. Jim Leach and staff are very personable, always available when I contact them and I couldn't ask for any better legal council than what I have. I am so Thankful I chose this "family" of legal knowlege to represent me.

Wayne and I are so happy we chose Jim Leach as our attorney to represent us in our malpractice case. The staff was a joy to work with and made us feel like part of the family. We were kept informed at each stage of the proceedings and were advised of our options. Hopefully you never find yourself in our situation, but if you do need an attorney, we highly recommend this firm. Jim, Vicki, Devyn, and all the other staff will take great care of you!
VERY AGRESSIVE ATTORNEY. ONE OF THE BEST IN THE BUSINESS.

He use to be a prosecutor and now he suppose to be a lawsuit attorney, but he is afraid to take 😏 cases with cops involved 🤔.  He waited to talk to me when my case's time ran out. He was very nasty to a hearing impaired person. (Me). His attitude 😒 needs to be a little bit more polite. If you must call 📞 when something happens call right away.  He is soooo snotty.

When I was in a wreck through no fault of my own in 2023 I went to Jim Leach. He explained everything in detail of what he will do and how the process worked. I researched all the accident attorneys before selecting Jim. His website is very detailed and gives you the information you need before you go in. When I first met him I was surprised at how down to earth he is and very personable. This is something you don’t find often in an attorney. He made me feel at ease. He took my case and began working on it immediately. He took all my information and gave me the emails and text numbers in case I needed a question answered. His staff is very helpful and when I asked a question they could not answer they would transfer me to Jim. If he was out of the office he would call me back within a couple hours either from the office or his cell. The care they put into my case was phenomenal. I would not have any other attorney should I need one again, (hopefully not). My case was settled in a little over a year although it does depend on your injuries as to how long it takes. If you’re ever in need of an accident attorney don’t go to anyone else. He is highly recommended and I will miss talking to him. The experience I had with them will be in my fondest memories forever. Thank you!

As a retiree and a careful driver, I never imagined that I would have to deal with the aftermath of a car wreck. Then I got rear-ended on a state highway by a driver who was traveling at highway speed. Although I was stiff and sore all over, the neck pain that started immediately was my biggest fear because I had already had a neck surgery a few years earlier, with a great outcome. I tried physical therapy and injections first because the last thing I wanted was another surgery. I fought it, but after two years, I had to agree to a second surgery.

It only took one conversation with the at-fault driver’s insurance company to realize I needed help. I called Jim Leach. He and his wonderful staff tracked everything, advised patience since I was not recovering from my neck injury, and eventually filed a lawsuit to protect my rights. As Jim predicted, the insurance company fought us about the second surgery, claiming it was due to my preexisting condition (even though my records showed a completely successful first surgery) and also said the gap between the wreck and my second surgery made the surgery unrelated. Jim set a trial date and advised me to have the courage to go to trial if necessary because the insurance company would try to make us believe they would never pay for my surgery from the wreck. Again, Jim advised patience, and he prepared my case for trial. Finally, about a month from trial, the insurance company agreed to pay.

I do not believe any other lawyer would have fought so hard for me or would have provided the kind of service that they did. Jim and Vicki and Kelley and Sydney and Devyn all were there for me. They made sure every medical bill was accounted for, paid my health insurance back, and even made sure every co-pay was at zero. I know that was a lot of work. I will always especially remember the mediation. Jim looked me in the eye and made me believe that he could, and would, take our case to a jury rather than accept the awful offers the insurance company had been making. In the end, he made a believer out of the insurance company as well, and I was grateful for a good settlement that did not require me to go to trial. Even after it was over, Jim took the time to discuss my experience with me and also advised me how to best protect myself under Ohio law with my own insurance coverages. I learned so much from this experience because Jim explained the way of everything.

I hope you never find yourself in a situation like mine, but if you do, call Jim. I know you will receive the same caring service that I did.

I was in an accident that was no fault of my own which left me with several medical bills due to the injuries. Jim and his team guided me through the steps of healing and got me a fair settlement in the end. I would recommend his services to everyone.

After a traumatic car crash that left me with permanent injuries and anxiety about driving, I called Jim Leach because he has a good reputation.  Jim and his excellent staff took over my claim and they took care of every detail of the case, which I came to learn is complex and even involved almost going to trial.  Through it all, they did not waver and were prepared to do whatever it took to achieve a fair outcome for me.  In fact, they greatly exceeded my expectations in the end.  In some ways, more important to me, was that they were always there for me.  They answered all my questions, helped me to understand the process, and Jim spent hours on the phone with me over the course of the case to reassure me that everything would be ok.  In a very difficult time of my life, I was very thankful that I went to Jim early on.  He and his entire staff are not only truly professionals, but they also cared a lot about me.  I give them 5 stars plus.

On a nice summer day, I rode my motorcycle to pick my daughter up from work near the Mall. We enjoyed these rides together, but on this day, after stopping at a stop sign, I started a left turn when a careless driver failed to see us and turned in to us in a t-bone hit. I stuck my leg out to protect my daughter at impact and my leg was severely broken, requiring reconstructive surgery and a long recovery. I will never have full use of my leg again. Fortunately, although my daughter was thrown from the bike and hurt, she did recover well.

I had heard good things about Jim Leach, so I called him. He took my call, did an investigation, and told me there was not enough coverage to pay for my injuries, but that he would help. Very few times in my life have I experienced the kind of selfless effort that Jim and his great staff put forward. They took care of every single detail for me, and for my daughter, and I will always be grateful to them for caring about us like they did. If you get hurt like I did, call Jim. He is a good guy who is easy to talk to and does what he says he will do.

We cannot say enough about Alan Pritt and his incredible team. After years of battling through a complicated adoption case filled with legal obstacles and errors from prior court proceedings, Mr. Pritt stood by our family every step of the way. His knowledge of the law, attention to detail, and commitment to doing what is right ultimately helped us achieve the outcome we had been fighting for.

What truly sets Mr. Pritt apart is not just his skill as an attorney, but his compassion. He understood how deeply personal this case was for our family and treated it with the seriousness and care it deserved. He worked diligently to correct past judicial mistakes and never lost focus on the goal.

His paralegal, Melissa, was equally outstanding. She consistently made herself available to answer questions, provide updates, and offer reassurance during some very stressful moments. Her professionalism, responsiveness, and kindness meant more to us than she probably realizes!

If you are looking for an attorney who is knowledgeable, persistent, professional, and genuinely cares about his clients, we highly recommend Alan Pritt and his team. We are forever grateful for everything they did for our family! We would recommend him to anyone!!!

reviews.text: I felt Secretary was rude
reviews.ownerResponse:
For posterity’s sake in case this review gets edited it says “I felt Secretary was rude”

First and foremost - we have zero clue who this person is. This isn’t a client name we’re aware of.

That being said, now, let’s break it down - this is a law firm that handles trench law. We handle family law, we handle criminal law, we handle abuse and neglect cases, wills, deeds, and guess what — we handle REALITY. Reality is, most people don’t like going into the court system if they can avoid it, and honestly do not usually trust other people as far as they can throw them. So, if you want to build a connection or trust with someone, you want someone up front who is not putting on a show or a facade. So, putting it out there here is the policy on this.

We follow the GOLDEN RULE: We treat others how they want to be treated, but importantly we do not sugarcoat for our clients if we can avoid it. That being said - we always try first and foremost to be friendly to and open to our clients while necessarily having to be truthful with them. Think of it like this, you ever call for customer support and they give you the run around with “I’m sorry you feel that way,” or ultimately feel they cannot answer your questions without some long-winded response? We try to avoid that - you have to be able to come to the table and help us to help your case, and sometimes that also means you have to deal with news that may be unpleasant to hear. We will be who you want us to try to be in court, but at the firm we have to be able to have open communication with you to actually ascertain your goals and properly represent you for what your aim of the case(s) are/is.

And just on the topic of this review itself, as of December 2025 we currently have two excellent secretaries: Penny and Isaac, both of whom at the time of this writing have been here for three years and approaching two years, respectively. In all of this time, there is only one instance that we can recall where a customer ever described either of them as rude, and you know what – we 100% back our secretary on this one. Why? Because if you are trying to become absolutely insufferable and intolerable where you start to belittle and be mean, we’re not going to tolerate that. This is a HIGHLY stressful time usually when going through Court, and we get that, but we’re all in this together. The Court staff from Wood, Jackson, Wirt, Ritchie, Calhoun, Roane, Tyler, Wetzel, Washington, Noble, and apologies for not saying the few others all know them and they have an excellent rapport with them. Whenever the attorneys have Court, and get the chance to ask them for a reflection it has always been positive feedback which gives us as the Attorneys a good feeling they are doing exactly what they need to do. We do believe the most important person in a law firm ultimately ends up being the Secretary because they are that central connection between attorney, court, and client as that first line of contact. Thus, while we feel bad you have this opinion, we highly believe it is wholly inaccurate. You just really can’t please everybody, no matter how hard you try.

reviews.text: My son was arrested for a DUI, during the first consultation, i made my biggest concern about him getting ANY jail time. He said the best way to get them to reduce or dismiss charges is by dragging out the proceedings. He never mentioned or corrected me about the avoiding jail time, it was a mandatory in the state of Ohio. My son ended up with arrest warrants issued because the attorneys office never informed us, and refused to take responsibility. We missed an earlier appointment because we were told one time that we needed to be there and the next time, we didnt. I dont know how many trips (2 hours round trip) that was some form of meeting or court appearance that we did try to be present only to find out that (parents & son) didnt habe to be present, this happened 3 times. But I still got to pay for his service,  I should have sent in a complaint to the Barr Association, because he did NOT tell me that the jail time was mandatory and no way to get out of that. He wasted our time and money, and was absolutely no help.

reviews.text:
Edited on August 27, 2025
Fair warning: It has been determined by the WV Office of Disciplinary Counsel that lawyers have broad discretion to exercise independent professional judgement. This determination was via a complaint that alleged overriding legal and ethical instructions, as well as documented fraudulent billing.

The fraudulent billing includes fabricated phone calls and inflated billable hours via (unopened) automatic emails from the court, all billed by Mr Summers himself. The firm's position is that "in accordance with the firm's standard engagement letter, he still was obligated to pay for services up to the date of termination." And yet to date, they have declined to pursue that alleged "obligation."

And finally, my experience with the firms attitude toward adoptive parents is questionable at best. I would highly recommend staying away from them.

I dealt with Adam McPherson,   He was very patient and thorough in handling my case.  My case involved a hearing injury from a vehicle accident.  So it wasn’t some straightforward, wham, bam thank you ma’am deal.  It took 11 months to complete the settlement but the results were outstanding and I got an excellent settlement.  So I would recommend letting these folks take the time they need to do their work.  It’ll be well worth it!!

Best PI lawyers anywhere.  Adam McPherson was knowledgeable, knew the ins and outs of insurance negotiation, and was flexible with me, letting me see doctors and specialists of my own choosing at my own convenience.  He got me a great settlement, but just as important is that I did not suffer with too many specialists, interviews, or stressful and wasteful activities. I was a victim of a vicious dog attack (60+ stitches and hospitalization), and initially contacted friends to see if anyone could recommend a good PI lawyer (oxymoron?). I had two recommendations – Kaufman & McPherson, and a larger firm in Charles Town (not Charleston) where I live. I interviewed both.  The Charles Town firm I did NOT select was big and professional, but even their proposed agreement with me smacked of greed.  They had a whole team of folks to “help me” and I am sure they would have made my life miserable running around to doctors and paperwork.  Perhaps this other firm could have gotten me a decent settlement, but I am sure they would have nickled and dimed me for expenses, taken a bigger cut, and made my life miserable. I should add that communication with Mr. M was prompt, efficient and professional whether by email or phone. Not to mention thoughtful and empathetic.  All lawyers (and anyone else) should run their business like Kaufman and McPherson.  I am so grateful for Adam McPherson.

I would highly recommend Chelsea to anyone needing help with the immigration process. Adjusting my status felt overwhelming, and I was worried about all the things that could potentially go wrong. From the very beginning, Chelsea explained everything clearly and in a way that was easy to understand, which immediately put us at ease.

Her expertise was evident throughout the entire process. She knew exactly what documents we needed and how to present our case in the strongest possible way. Thanks to her guidance and attention to detail, we felt confident every step of the way and ultimately, we were approved.

Whenever I had questions, Chelsea always made time to respond calmly and thoroughly. She has a reassuring presence and genuinely cares about her clients. She’s also incredibly easy to talk to and makes you feel comfortable, like you’ve known her for years.

Beyond her professionalism, Chelsea was very personable and truly seemed to care about what was going on in our lives outside of just the paperwork. She took the time to check in, listen, and make us feel like more than just another case file. That personal touch made a stressful process feel much more manageable.

We’re so grateful for her help and couldn’t have asked for a better attorney.

The best decision I could have made during a very stressful time. I contacted Chelsea to file and handle my immigration forms and she did an amazing job. She was very communicative and responded quickly to every question or concern I had. She is also very detail oriented and will pay attention anything that might have been missed by you or overlooked. She handles cases with care and will give you the best chance at getting the desired outcome. The fees associated were also fair and very reasonable. I would hire her services again in the future if I needed them and would also highly recommend to friends as well.

Thank you again for your help Chelsea, wishing you all the best!

Chelsea is not only very experienced with the immigration process, but she also cares deeply about your success and your win. She is veteran-friendly, approachable, and clearly committed to getting the best outcome for her clients—from beginning to end.

reviews.text: Great guy. He does a good job at court as well.

I had an extremely disappointing experience with this lawyer and guardian ad litem. Despite what he may think of his own intentions, his arrogance and preconceived judgments without the facts proved to be detrimental. His absolute refusal to listen after he has decided “he knows what’s going on” is sad. His lack of organization and rude behavior added to the frustration. Politeness seemed reserved only for those with political influence or in his good graces.  Jeff’s gullibility was disheartening – he readily believed well-told lies without considering the truth from others.  In this case a child paid for his incompetence, until the truth revealed itself later.  My advice: steer clear at all costs; it's an experience I would not wish on anyone.
He treated my son very poorly on the phone and interrupted him several times. Made sure to point out that he will charge him if he filed for contempt against his ex-wife. ALL ABOUT THE MONEY.

I called to discuss how I was worried that my ex-wife (his client) was breaking the court order by spending time with her boyfriend at a hotel with my daughter. In our court documents, her current boyfriend is not allowed to stay under the same roof or in the same bed (that’s my assumption at least, he hasn’t given me the papers so I can be 100% sure which is why I called). I called to talk with him about this and was treated very poorly and was interrupted by him saying, “So? So? So?” And then he proceeded to tell me that if I filed for contempt that he would charge me $350 for attorney fees EVEN THOUGH I am just trying to protect my daughter from what I thought Judge VanMeter agreed to. I would highly not recommend him simply for his aggressive behavior when I was being completely polite and reasonable. He cares only about the money and it shows, especially since he had 10 days to file the papers for court for me to review and sign and the 10 days was on 11/3 (and that’s business days.). The court date was 10/23/23 and I am going to make sure my friends and family knows how poorly I was treated.

I called Jeff on a whim as I was quickly losing hope for finding someone to represent me in a divorce/ custody case. Before I had even agreed to retain him as my attorney, he was looking at his calendar and planning to move other obligations around so that he could be with me for representation at mediation. He has been in my corner and supported me, corrected me, and empowered me from the beginning and my only regret is that I hadn’t found him before my first hearing. I could not recommend Jeff more. He is honest, humorous, humble and fierce - everything you need when you feel like you’re against the ropes.

Ron Walters is an absolutely phenomenal attorney. He is brilliant, caring, and knows the law, and its practical implications, very thoroughly. Ron was a law school classmate of mine and he was a super star even in law school. So, when I stumbled upon late and nonpaying tenants in my beloved family home, I hired him and Ron was there for me every step of the way. He answered my many questions and assuaged my anxieties… much more than he needed to. He also knew the functional law so well, and his experiences and excellent record gave me much certainty in my case. I am forever grateful, and cannot recommend Ron Walters enough. He’s the guy you want in your corner when you need a great lawyer.

I’ve known Ron for years dating back to our time at WVU as undergrads. He’s been a long time friend, and I’ve always been impressed with his financial knowledge. So I wasn’t shocked when he went to law school and got his JD.

That said, I hired Ron to help me with my Ch. 7 BK case last year. I was way in over my head, and after trying to work with lenders, I concluded that bankruptcy was my only option. I met with Ron for a free consultation, and while it nice to see and catch up with an old friend, I was in dire need of help.

Ron agreed to take my case on. I paid his full fee over several payments. Sure, there were a few bumps in the road, but the bankruptcy petition was eventually filed. He prepped me for the meeting of creditors and the bankruptcy trustee. Meeting went very smoothly. Several months later, a successful discharge.

I’m doing much better financially these days, and the biggest piece of advice Ron gave me was to “be patient” when it came to rebuilding my finances. I’ve had to remind myself of that several times.

I highly recommend that if anyone is in financial dire straits, call Ron’s office!

Ron & Bev have been amazing in every aspect of us needing them. Whatever it may be, they & the entire firm have been a helping hand. They have helped us succeed in so many ways. All the 1* reviews on this site and on Tabor Lindsay & Associates’ Facebook page were not posted by the law firm’s clients or anyone that has an affiliation with the firm.  Instead, the reviews were posted in response to an article concerning a scientific research study on the possible health risks of E-cigarettes that was posted on Tabor Lindsay’s Facebook page.  No one from the firm posted comments regarding the use, non-use, or hazards of the use of E-cigarettes.  The law firm has no financial interest in the study or any litigation regarding the subject matter.  The article was posted as part of an effort to provide the public new and interesting health news.  Due to the inappropriate comments and the use of unacceptable language, the Facebook posting was deleted.  The doctor/lawyers, lawyers, and staff of Tabor Lindsay & Associates are dedicated to the protection of the rights and safety of patients and their families as they navigate the medical system.  The firm will not be deterred from sharing pertinent health news and legal information with the community.

Tabor Lindsay law firm can help if you feel you have been wronged by a healthcare provider. I highly respect this firm and would recommend contacting them for any legal needs you may have . Wrongful death cases against the federal government arising from malpractice require first submitting the claim under the Federal Tort Claims Act (FTCA), a time-consuming process. Few lawyers have the expertise in medical malpractice and the FTCA to get the results that we do. We care about our clients and their families. The loss of a loved one is never easy, especially when it should not have happened and was totally preventable. We never forget the human side of the case. reviews.text:
I was injured in a wreck with a tractor trailer and felt overwhelmed. On top of my injuries and the stress associated with that, the whole process left me anxious and unsure where to turn. After speaking with several attorneys, a friend recommended Tony O’Dell of Tiano O’Dell, and from my very first conversation with him I knew he was the right match for our family.

Tony and his staff (Ashley was wonderful!) were professional, caring, and reassuring from start to finish. He never let up in pursuing not only my well-being but also making sure we were not taken advantage of. The process was lengthy, but that was because Tony stood and fought until we reached a fair settlement that took care of us.

I’m very thankful we found Tony O’Dell and Tiano O’Dell. He is a real professional who was always available at any hour and guided us through one of the most stressful and difficult times in our lives, and I would absolutely recommend him to anyone in need of an accident and injury lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Matt, thank you so much for the great review. It means a lot to us. Tractor trailer cases are not like typical car wreck cases. Trucking companies and their insurance companies have teams of experts that need to be neutralized by lawyers who know their way around the trucking rules and regulations that trucking companies must follow. We held tight to your trial date and the trucking company, and its insurer, folded before we got to a WV jury because they knew we were prepared to take them down. Good lawyers communicate with their clients and help alleviate their fears and concerns, which naturally come with being unfamiliar with the process. reviews.text: The team at this law firm is nothing short of amazing. Ashley is always so polite and helps clients get whatever they need, she truly holds the place down. He will fight for his clients until he cannot anymore, and he is so appreciated. He is very thorough with his clients and explains everything in great detail so that they understand each aspect of the case. I will always recommend this firm to anyone in need.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you very much for the wonderful and thoughtful review, Kami. We bond with our clients regardless of whether it is an auto collision, medical malpractice or some other type of injury or accident. We treat everybody the way we would want our family to be treated. It means a lot when clients feel that and the take the time to tell others about their experience.  Individual attention to detail and one-on-one treatment is not a given, especially for this firms that must hurry cases along to pay for their huge advertising budgets, but at Tiano O'Dell it is. Maximizing recovery and making sure the client is comfortable and well-informed are the way we do business. Thank you again.

reviews.text: They were so helpful in guiding me and my family through the process with my teenage daughter's motor vehicle accident. reviews.text:
Hannah Tothe and Pence Law Firm have been an absolute blessing to me and my family. Hannah did not just handle my case. She stood beside us through every stressful moment. She treated me with respect, talked to me like a real person, and made sure I always understood what was happening.

What meant the most was how she fought for my child like he was her own. Hannah made sure I knew that I was not carrying the weight of my case alone. She brought hope and steadiness at a time when I truly needed it.

In court she is confident and composed, and outside of court she is the kind of person who checks on how you are holding up, not just how the case is going.

I am genuinely grateful for everything she and Pence Law Firm have done. If you want an attorney and a team who feel like family and truly show up for you, Hannah Tothe and Pence Law Firm are the ones.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are deeply grateful for your review and the trust you placed in Hannah Tothe and Pence Law Firm. Thank you for recognizing Hannah's dedication and for letting us be a source of support for your family.

reviews.text:
I would be remiss not to express my deep disappointment in how I’ve been treated, in my dealings with attorney Anna Adkins. At times, I felt more like a burden than a client, and that sense of dismissal only compounded the stress of an already difficult situation. I came into this process hoping for advocacy and support. Instead, I was often left feeling unheard, unimportant, and emotionally sidelined.

Professionalism includes not only legal expertise, but also the ability to communicate with empathy and respect. Unfortunately, that has not consistently been my experience. I expected representation rooted in a genuine effort to understand my story and protect my interests - what I received often felt like indifference.

I sincerely hope that future clients are treated with more care, respect, and attentiveness than I was. Everyone facing a legal battle deserves to feel that their voice matters and that their advocate truly stands beside them.

I do however want to say a giant thank you to the receptionist Ashley, who always handled my calls with grace and kindness.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. I'm sorry to hear that your experience with our office did not meet your expectations. We are committed to providing every client with respect, attention, and professionalism throughout their legal journey. Your feedback has been noted, and we’re always working to improve how we communicate and support those we serve. I’m also glad to hear that Ashley made a positive impression—she’s a valued part of our team. We wish you all the best moving forward.

I have no idea why it would cost 1500 to talk to the magistrate or to have a letter sent to the ex and her lawyer. I am a retired attorney who practiced with Ms Francis for a number of years.  In my experience, she exhibited the highest standards of practice, in both her understanding of the law and in her exceptional treatment of her clients.  I referred many folks to her for representation following my retirement.  I would not hesitate to continue to refer folks to her now.  As for the lone individual who objects to Ms Francis as an attorney, I think the basis for her complaint is that there has been no hearing regarding child custody.  If the Court continues and resets a hearing based on the request of the opposing counsel or by the order of the Court itself, that is beyond the power of Ms Francis. Family Court issues are the most important issues in the court system, and it is understandable that parties to a case become frustrated, but it makes no sense to blame your attorney when a continuance occurs. I would encourage anyone with a family legal matter to entrust your case to Ms Francis.
Ms. Francis cares about her clients. She is curteoys, professional, and honest. She has represented me and I recommend her.

On my interview, I had brought new evidences and when i told him I got more evidences, all he had to say was dont worry because the ones we sent earlier is enough and shut me down. 🤦🏾Damn cocky!!! . After the interview, I knew there was gonna be a NOID from USCIS and I was right. He charged me again and responded. Unfortunately, I was denied with a very lame reason. My wife asked that we appeal but I was pissed to have another son of an immigrant suck my blood so I decided to let this pass and moved on.
I am saying it today because I think some vampires are out there who just want blood from innocent people. I owe my immigrant community not to fall for it.

I decided to trust Cary Law Office after reading the Google reviews, and I’m really glad I took that chance because they were absolutely right. From the very first conversation, you can tell that they genuinely care about their clients and the people in the community. A lot of law firms make you feel like you’re just another case file, but that is absolutely not the experience here. You’re treated with respect, patience, and real attention.

What impressed me most was the level of communication. Every question I had was answered clearly, and I never felt like I was being rushed off the phone. Legal situations can be stressful and confusing, but Cary Law Office takes the time to explain things in a way that actually makes sense. They keep you updated, they return calls, and they make sure you understand what’s happening every step of the way.

Another thing that stands out is how hard they fight for their clients. It’s obvious that they take their work seriously and truly want the best possible outcome for the people they represent. You can tell there is a lot of experience and knowledge behind what they do, but they also bring a level of compassion that you don’t always see in the legal world.

It’s also clear that Cary Law Office cares deeply about the community. They’re not just practicing law—they’re invested in helping people and making a real difference.

If you’re looking for a law firm that will treat you like a person, keep you informed, and work hard on your behalf, this is the place to go. Don’t waste your time looking anywhere else. Cary Law Office sets the standard for how clients should be treated.
